name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 09.02.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 09.02.2026.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 30, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 676.84 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 96.20 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 660.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 30, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 676.84 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 96.20 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 660.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum  temperatures  are likely to  be  below  normal  in  East  India,  East M.P., Vidarbha, Marathwada, East U.P., some parts of NE India and many parts of South India. Minimum temperatures is likely to be above normal in many parts of NW India, Gujarat, Madhya Maharashtra and some parts of South Karnataka.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum  temperatures  are likely to  be  below  normal  in  East  India,  East M.P., Vidarbha, Marathwada, East U.P., some parts of NE India and many parts of South India. Minimum temperatures is likely to be above normal in many parts of NW India, Gujarat, Madhya Maharashtra and some parts of South Karnataka.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- February 08 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  41%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  89%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  23%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  79%;  and South Peninsula: (-) 6%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- February 08 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  41%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  89%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  23%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  79%;  and South Peninsula: (-) 6%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Under  the  influence  of  a  Western  Disturbance,  isolated  to  scattered  rainfall/snowfall likely  over  Western  Himalayan  region  during  9th  -  11th  February  and  no  significant weather likely over rest parts of the country during next 7 days.
texts.text:  Under  the  influence  of  a  Western  Disturbance,  isolated  to  scattered  rainfall/snowfall likely  over  Western  Himalayan  region  during  9th  -  11th  February  and  no  significant weather likely over rest parts of the country during next 7 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A  wet  spell  with  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall/snowfall  likely  over  Jammu-KashmirLadakh-Gilgit-BaltistanMuzaffarabad,  Himachal Pradesh  and  Uttarakhand during  9th  11th and isolated rainfall/snowfall likely over same region on 14th & 15th February with Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall/snowfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty winds likely over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad and isolated to scattered over Himachal Pradesh on 10th February.
texts.text:  A  wet  spell  with  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall/snowfall  likely  over  Jammu-KashmirLadakh-Gilgit-BaltistanMuzaffarabad,  Himachal Pradesh  and  Uttarakhand during  9th  11th and isolated rainfall/snowfall likely over same region on 14th & 15th February with Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall/snowfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty winds likely over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad and isolated to scattered over Himachal Pradesh on 10th February.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall is likely over J&K, Himachal Pradesh and isolated pockets of Uttarakhand.
texts.text:  Rainfall is likely over J&K, Himachal Pradesh and isolated pockets of Uttarakhand.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense to very dense conditions likely during morning/night hours at isolated places over East Uttar Pradesh till 10th February.
texts.text:  Dense to very dense conditions likely during morning/night hours at isolated places over East Uttar Pradesh till 10th February.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  05  February  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  122.313  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  67%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 108.49 percent of last  year's storage  and 124.97 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  05  February  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  122.313  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  67%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 108.49 percent of last  year's storage  and 124.97 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Minimum Temperature is likely to  be  below  normal in many parts of East  India,  Central India,  South India, many parts of Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Punjab and many parts of West Rajasthan.
texts.text:  Minimum Temperature is likely to  be  below  normal in many parts of East  India,  Central India,  South India, many parts of Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Punjab and many parts of West Rajasthan.
